message:
docs: add evidence pack workflow to README

Add comprehensive documentation for evidence pack CLI:
- CLI command map: 7 new evidence commands
- Workflow section: step-by-step guide with worked example
- Evidence types table
- Artifact citation syntax examples
- File layout diagram
- Link to full spec

Closes brenner_bot-5so.13.2.4

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

create evidence pack - cite it in artifacts # Acceptance criteria - [ ] README/runbook includes at least one worked example ","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:08:15.940987058-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-01T14:35:02.31872787-05:00","labels":["docs","evidence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.2.4","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:08:15.961583894-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.2.4","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:13:47.587031971-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.2.5","title":"Task: Add CLI evidence post helper (render + send Agent Mail message)","description":"# Goal Reduce friction for using evidence packs in real sessions by providing a **one-command** helper to publish evidence into the Agent Mail thread. --- # Why this matters If evidence lives only on disk, agents won’t see it unless the operator manually copy/pastes. The easiest low-friction workflow is: 1) build/update evidence pack on disk 2) render a clean markdown summary 3) post it into the thread as an `EVIDENCE:` message --- # Proposed UX ```bash # Render + send in one step ./brenner.ts evidence post \ --thread-id RS-... \ --sender Operator \ --to BlueLake,PurpleMountain,RedForest \ --evidence-id EV-001,EV-002 ``` Behavior: - renders the selected evidence records (or all by default) - sends via Agent Mail with a stable subject prefix (e.g. `EVIDENCE:`) --- # Acceptance criteria - [ ] Command exists (or subcommand under `evidence`) - [ ] Output is deterministic and suitable for thread context - [ ] Clear failure modes (missing evidence id, missing pack) ","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownSnow","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:44:01.476600248-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T04:08:28.108991459-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T04:08:28.108991459-05:00","close_reason":"Added evidence post command to render + send evidence packs as EVIDENCE messages, with a dry-run mode and CLI tests.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.2.5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:44:01.477886919-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.2.5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:44:12.016579362-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.3","title":"Feature: Artifact schema + linter support evidence pack citations","description":"# Goal Allow canonical artifacts and deltas to reference external evidence records (per `brenner_bot-aly1`) with stable IDs. --- # Requirements - Artifact schema spec documents allowed citation/reference formats: - evidence IDs: `EV-001` - evidence anchors: `EV-001#E1` - Delta format optionally supports `evidence_refs` on relevant operations. - Linter validates: - reference syntax is well-formed - (optionally) referenced evidence IDs exist in the session evidence pack --- # Acceptance criteria - [ ] Artifacts can include evidence references without being flagged invalid - [ ] Linter catches malformed evidence refs - [ ] Renderer preserves evidence refs verbatim ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:08:28.923623194-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-01T14:30:18.035170789-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-01T14:30:18.035170789-05:00","close_reason":"All 4 subtasks completed: artifact schema updated (5so.13.3.1), linter accepts evidence citations (5so.13.3.2), citation parsing tests added (5so.13.3.3), role prompt templates updated (5so.13.3.4)","labels":["artifact-schema","citations","lint"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:08:28.944011036-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:08:28.949721667-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.3.1","title":"Task: Update artifact schema spec for evidence citations","description":"# Goal Update the protocol documentation so artifact writers know how to cite topic evidence. --- # Likely change - Either bump schema to v0.2, or add an additive appendix to v0.1. - Document citation formats: - Brenner: `§n` - Evidence pack: `S1.E2` - (optional) code citations --- # Acceptance criteria - [ ] Spec includes examples and provenance rules ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:08:41.265949601-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T00:03:57.844277144-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T00:03:57.844277144-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented via evidence-anchor section (EV-001 / EV-001#E1) with examples + provenance rule; landed in commit b309167.","labels":["artifact-schema","citations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.3.1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:08:41.28580959-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} @@ -494,7 +494,7 @@ {"id":"brenner_bot-uwe","title":"Task: Implement result highlighting","description":"Highlight matched terms in search results. ## Purpose Highlighting shows users WHY a result matched, helping them quickly identify relevant results. ## Requirements ### In Search Results - Matched terms highlighted in snippet - Distinct visual treatment (bold, color, background) - Must be accessible (not color-only) ### In Document (after navigation) - Scroll matched section into view - Temporary highlight effect (fade after 2-3 seconds) - Clear visual indicator of matched content ## Implementation Approach ### Snippet Highlighting 1. Search engine returns match positions 2. Component wraps matched text in or styled span 3. Style with background and/or bold ### Document Highlighting 1. URL includes search query parameter 2. Document page detects query param 3. Scroll to first match 4. Apply temporary highlight class 5. Remove highlight after timeout ## Styling ```css .search-highlight { background: var(--primary) / 20%; border-radius: 2px; padding: 0 2px; } .temp-highlight { animation: highlight-fade 2s ease-out; } @keyframes highlight-fade { 0% { background: var(--primary) / 40%; } 100% { background: transparent; } } ``` ## Accessibility - Don't rely on color alone - Add sr-only text if needed: 'matched term' - Ensure sufficient contrast ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Matches highlighted in result snippets - [ ] Highlighting is accessible (not color-only) - [ ] After navigation, match scrolled into view - [ ] Temporary highlight on matched section - [ ] Highlight fades naturally - [ ] Works in both light and dark mode","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:30:25.154597345-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:05:35.099071119-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:05:35.099071119-05:00","close_reason":"HighlightedText component in SpotlightSearch highlights matched terms in snippets. Post-navigation scroll-to-match can be a separate follow-up task.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-uwe","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-d4p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:31:11.947646525-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-uwe","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-dy2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:31:12.082200405-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-v2e","title":"Build native React document renderer with custom components","description":"# Task Build a rich document renderer using NATIVE React/TypeScript components that fully leverage Tailwind and our design system. NOT a generic markdown viewer! # Philosophy The content deserves custom treatment: - Brenner quotes should look *amazing* (not just gray italic blocks) - Operator symbols (⊘, 𝓛, ≡, ✂) should be styled distinctively - Code blocks should use our OKLCH colors - Tables should be interactive, not HTML tables - § anchors should be beautiful and functional # Architecture ``` Markdown source → MDX/AST parser → Custom React components ``` 1. Parse markdown at build time (or server-side) 2. Map each AST node to a custom component 3. Each component uses our design system # Custom Components Needed ## Typography - `...` - `...` - `...` (intro paragraphs) ## Brenner-specific - `...` - Special quote styling with gradient border, attribution - `` - Styled operator with tooltip - `` - Animated anchor with copy functionality ## Content blocks - `...` - Syntax highlighting with our colors - `` - Interactive table with sorting, hover - `...` - Styled callout boxes - `...` - Glossary-style definitions ## Interactive - `` - Copy citation/text - `` - Share to social/clipboard - `...` # Implementation Use `next-mdx-remote` or `@mdx-js/mdx` to: 1. Parse markdown to AST 2. Provide custom components mapping 3. Render with our components ```tsx import { MDXRemote } from 'next-mdx-remote/rsc'; const components = { h1: (props) => , blockquote: (props) => , // ... }; ``` # Quote Styling Example ```tsx function BrennerQuote({ children, source }) { return ( " {children} {source && ( — Sydney Brenner, {source} )} ); } ``` # TOC Generation Still generate TOC from headings, but render with our components: - Sticky sidebar on desktop - Collapsible on mobile - Scroll-spy highlighting - Smooth scroll on click # Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All markdown elements map to custom components - [ ] Typography uses our design system - [ ] Quotes look beautiful (not generic) - [ ] Operators have distinctive styling - [ ] § anchors are interactive - [ ] TOC works with scroll-spy - [ ] Animations respect reduced motion - [ ] Responsive layout","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:14:46.390974262-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.455541735-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.455541735-05:00","close_reason":"Superseded by brenner_bot-5so.4.1.1 + brenner_bot-5so.4.1.2","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-v2e","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:16:24.931287472-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-v2e","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-y6z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:06.908825369-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-v2e","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-wpb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:06.941047723-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-v41w","title":"Task: Create Transition History Logging","description":"# Task: Create Transition History Logging ## Objective Implement persistent logging of all state transitions for audit trail and history queries. ## Implementation Details ### File Location `apps/web/src/lib/hypothesis-history.ts` ### Storage Structure ``` .research/ └── transitions/ ├── RS-20251230-transitions.json # Per-session └── ... ``` ### File Format ```json { "sessionId": "RS-20251230", "transitions": [ { "hypothesisId": "H-RS20251230-001", "fromState": "proposed", "toState": "active", "trigger": "activate", "triggeredBy": "user", "timestamp": "2025-12-30T10:05:00Z" }, { "hypothesisId": "H-RS20251230-001", "fromState": "active", "toState": "killed", "trigger": "kill", "triggeredBy": "T-RS20251230-002", "reason": "ATAC-seq showed open chromatin, contradicting methylation-only hypothesis", "timestamp": "2025-12-31T15:30:00Z" } ] } ``` ### Core Functions ```typescript export async function logTransition(transition: StateTransition): Promise export async function getHypothesisHistory(hypothesisId: string): Promise export async function getSessionTransitions(sessionId: string): Promise export async function getTransitionsInRange( start: Date, end: Date ): Promise export interface HypothesisTimeline { hypothesisId: string; statement: string; transitions: Array<{ fromState: HypothesisState; toState: HypothesisState; trigger: TransitionTrigger; timestamp: string; triggeredBy?: string; }>; currentState: HypothesisState; } export async function buildHypothesisTimeline( hypothesisId: string ): Promise ``` ### Query Examples ```typescript // "What killed hypothesis H1?" const history = await getHypothesisHistory('H-RS20251230-001'); const killTransition = history.find(t => t.toState === 'killed'); // → T-RS20251230-002 killed it at 2025-12-31T15:30:00Z // "What happened in session RS-20251230?" const transitions = await getSessionTransitions('RS-20251230'); // → [activate H1, activate H2, kill H1, ...] ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All transitions logged to per-session files - [ ] Query by hypothesis ID - [ ] Query by session ID - [ ] Query by time range - [ ] Timeline reconstruction shows full history - [ ] Append-only log (no editing history) ## Dependencies - Task: Define State Transition Types ## Effort Estimate ~150 lines of code, ~2 hours","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:05.370418529-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T12:04:03.696867615-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T12:04:03.696867615-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented in apps/web/src/lib/schemas/hypothesis-lifecycle.ts: transitionHypothesis function (line 292), TransitionHistoryStore class (line 469), and 72 passing unit tests. All acceptance criteria met.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-v41w","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-6n4e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:46:53.172057824-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-v41w","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-slcc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:46:53.328944504-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-v41w","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:41:08.514542199-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} -{"id":"brenner_bot-vmsb","title":"EPIC: Testing Infrastructure & Full Coverage (No Mocks)","description":"# Goal Achieve comprehensive test coverage using **real implementations** where possible. # Mocking Philosophy (CLARIFIED) **✅ DO test with real implementations:** - Internal modules (parsers, state management, utilities) - DOM rendering via happy-dom - React hooks and components - Data transformations and business logic **✅ ACCEPTABLE to mock/stub:** - `framer-motion` - Animation timing causes test flakiness - External network calls in **unit tests** (not E2E) - for isolation - Browser APIs not available in test env (IntersectionObserver, etc.) **❌ DO NOT mock:** - Internal module dependencies (import real modules) - Component children (render real children) - State management (use real stores) # Current State (2025-12-30) - **Unit tests**: 41 files, 50% coverage threshold - **E2E tests**: 5 spec files - **Logging**: Excellent infrastructure exists # Critical Gaps **Unit Tests Missing:** - 16+ UI components (SpotlightSearch, DistillationViewer, SessionForm, nav, etc.) - Library modules (corpus.ts, prompt-builder.ts, search/*) **E2E Gaps:** - Search workflow (MAIN USER JOURNEY - untested!) - Session lifecycle, error handling, auth flows # Acceptance Criteria - [ ] 80% line coverage (up from 50%) - [ ] All interactive components tested with real DOM - [ ] All critical user journeys have E2E coverage - [ ] CI fails on coverage regression # Structure - **Unit Tests**: Real DOM, real state, real parsers - **E2E Tests**: Real application, real data, detailed logging - **Infrastructure**: Fixtures, coverage enforcement, accessibility","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:49:24.662121237-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T22:43:54.160607467-05:00","labels":["testing quality"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-vmsb","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:35:55.792252655-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} +{"id":"brenner_bot-vmsb","title":"EPIC: Testing Infrastructure & Full Coverage (No Mocks)","description":"# Goal Achieve comprehensive test coverage using **real implementations** where possible. # Mocking Philosophy (CLARIFIED) **✅ DO test with real implementations:** - Internal modules (parsers, state management, utilities) - DOM rendering via happy-dom - React hooks and components - Data transformations and business logic **✅ ACCEPTABLE to mock/stub:** - `framer-motion` - Animation timing causes test flakiness - External network calls in **unit tests** (not E2E) - for isolation - Browser APIs not available in test env (IntersectionObserver, etc.) **❌ DO NOT mock:** - Internal module dependencies (import real modules) - Component children (render real children) - State management (use real stores) # Current State (2025-12-30) - **Unit tests**: 41 files, 50% coverage threshold - **E2E tests**: 5 spec files - **Logging**: Excellent infrastructure exists # Critical Gaps **Unit Tests Missing:** - 16+ UI components (SpotlightSearch, DistillationViewer, SessionForm, nav, etc.) - Library modules (corpus.ts, prompt-builder.ts, search/*) **E2E Gaps:** - Search workflow (MAIN USER JOURNEY - untested!) - Session lifecycle, error handling, auth flows # Acceptance Criteria - [ ] 80% line coverage (up from 50%) - [ ] All interactive components tested with real DOM - [ ] All critical user journeys have E2E coverage - [ ] CI fails on coverage regression # Structure - **Unit Tests**: Real DOM, real state, real parsers - **E2E Tests**: Real application, real data, detailed logging - **Infrastructure**: Fixtures, coverage enforcement, accessibility","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:49:24.662121237-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-01T14:34:47.75397559-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-01T14:34:47.75397559-05:00","close_reason":"All children completed: Test Infrastructure Enhancements (4ep3), Unit Test Coverage (8dy3), E2E Integration Tests (hvi7). Coverage thresholds enforced, real implementations used, CI integration complete.","labels":["testing quality"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-vmsb","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:35:55.792252655-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-voa","title":"TanStack Query: Migrate Document Viewers to Query Hooks","description":"# Goal Migrate TranscriptViewer and DistillationViewer to use the new Query hooks instead of receiving data as props from Server Components. ## Background Currently, document viewers receive pre-fetched data as props: ```typescript // TranscriptViewer.tsx interface TranscriptViewerProps { data: TranscriptData; // Passed from Server Component } ``` This works but means: 1. Data is re-fetched on every navigation 2. No caching between page visits 3. Server must parse on every request ## Migration Strategy ### Option A: Keep Server Fetch + Hydrate (Recommended) Server Component prefetches, passes to client via HydrationBoundary. - Best for SEO (content in initial HTML) - Best for performance (no client-side loading flash) - Query still provides caching for subsequent navigations ### Option B: Full Client-Side Fetch Remove server prefetch, let Query handle everything. - Simpler code - Shows loading state on first visit - Worse SEO **We choose Option A** because the corpus pages are content-heavy and benefit from SSR. ## Implementation ### 1. Update corpus/[doc]/page.tsx ```typescript import { HydrationBoundary, QueryClient, dehydrate } from '@tanstack/react-query'; export default async function CorpusDocPage({ params }) { const queryClient = new QueryClient(); await queryClient.prefetchQuery({ queryKey: ['corpus', 'doc', params.doc], queryFn: () => readCorpusDoc(params.doc), }); return ( ); } ``` ### 2. Update TranscriptViewer ```typescript export function TranscriptViewer({ docId }: { docId: string }) { const { data, isLoading, error } = useCorpusDoc(docId); if (isLoading) return ; if (error) return ; // ... rest of component } ``` ### 3. Update DistillationViewer similarly ## Considerations ### Skeleton Components Create loading skeletons that match the layout: - TranscriptSkeleton: TOC + content placeholders - DistillationSkeleton: Header + section placeholders ### Error Boundaries Create error UI for: - Document not found - Parse failure - Network error ### Backward Compatibility During migration, support both patterns: - Accept data as prop (old pattern) - Use Query if docId provided (new pattern) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] TranscriptViewer uses Query hook - [ ] DistillationViewer uses Query hook - [ ] SSR still works (content in initial HTML) - [ ] Navigation between docs is instant (cached) - [ ] Loading states are graceful - [ ] Error states are handled ## Files to Modify - apps/web/src/app/corpus/[doc]/page.tsx - apps/web/src/components/transcript/TranscriptViewer.tsx - apps/web/src/components/distillation/DistillationViewer.tsx ## Files to Create - apps/web/src/components/transcript/TranscriptSkeleton.tsx - apps/web/src/components/distillation/DistillationSkeleton.tsx","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:15:54.657062985-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:48:57.870114122-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T23:48:57.870114122-05:00","close_reason":"Migrated document viewers to TanStack Query hooks with HydrationBoundary for SSR prefetching and navigation caching. Created DocumentContentClient.tsx client component that uses useCorpusDoc hook.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-voa","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1r5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:16:17.466921916-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-vtz","title":"Bug: install.sh --dry-run should not edit shell config","description":"In install.sh, configure_path currently appends PATH lines to shell rc when --easy-mode is set even if --dry-run is true. Dry-run should be side-effect free. Also avoid set -u crashes when SHELL is unset; warn + return.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-30T04:30:33.336980724-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T04:33:37.461190888-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T04:33:37.461190888-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed install.sh configure_path to be side-effect free in --dry-run, and guarded unset SHELL under set -u.","labels":["installer"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-vtz","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.2.4","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-30T04:30:33.341541068-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-vxd3","title":"Feature: Anomaly Registry","description":"# Feature: Anomaly Registry ## Purpose Track anomalies (surprising observations) as first-class entities with quarantine status and resolution tracking. This fills a CRITICAL gap and enables proper paradox grounding. ## Why This Is Needed The Brenner method STARTS with paradoxes: - §103: "Both could be wrong" — anomalies reveal when framings are inadequate - The artifact schema Section 6 is the **Anomaly Register** - The evaluation rubric has "Anomaly Quarantine Discipline" (0-3) as a scored dimension - The scorecard's "Paradox Grounding" dimension requires anomaly data! Current gap: Anomalies exist only as text in artifacts. We can't: - Track which anomalies are still unresolved - Link anomalies to hypotheses that attempt to explain them - Answer "what surprising observations drove this research?" From §147: "Exclusion is always a tremendously good thing" — but you can't exclude if you don't track what you're excluding! ## Schema Design ```typescript export const QuarantineStatusSchema = z.enum([ 'active', // Anomaly not yet resolved 'resolved', // Explained by a hypothesis (link to which one) 'deferred', // Parked until core question settled 'paradigm_shifting', // So significant it changes the research thread ]); export const AnomalySchema = z.object({ /** Stable ID: X-{session_id}-{sequence} */ id: z.string().regex(/^X-[A-Z0-9]+-\d{3}$/), /** What was observed that doesn't fit? */ observation: z.string().min(10), /** Source: where did this observation come from? */ source: z.object({ type: z.enum(['experiment', 'literature', 'discussion', 'calculation']), reference: z.string().optional(), // Paper, session ID, etc. anchors: z.array(z.string()).optional(), // §n anchors }), /** What does this conflict with? */ conflictsWith: z.object({ hypotheses: z.array(z.string()), // H-xxx IDs that this challenges assumptions: z.array(z.string()), // A-xxx IDs that this challenges description: z.string(), // Human explanation of conflict }), /** Current quarantine status */ quarantineStatus: QuarantineStatusSchema, /** Resolution plan or deferral reason */ resolutionPlan: z.string().optional(), /** If resolved, which hypothesis explains it? */ resolvedBy: z.string().optional(), // H-xxx ID /** Can this anomaly spawn new hypotheses? */ spawnedHypotheses: z.array(z.string()).optional(), // H-xxx IDs it generated /** Session where this was recorded */ sessionId: z.string(), /** Timestamps */ createdAt: z.string().datetime(), updatedAt: z.string().datetime(), /** Free-form notes */ notes: z.string().optional(), }); ``` ## Anomaly Lifecycle ``` ┌─────────────┐ │ active │ (default state) └──────┬──────┘ │ ┌──────────────┼──────────────┐ │ │ │ ▼ ▼ ▼ ┌─────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐ ┌────────────────────┐ │ resolved │ │ deferred │ │ paradigm_shifting │ └─────────────┘ └─────────────┘ └────────────────────┘ │ ▼ [May spawn new RT] ``` ## Anomaly Quarantine Discipline From the evaluation rubric (0-3): - 0: Anomalies ignored or used to prematurely kill frameworks - 1: Anomalies mentioned but not formally quarantined - 2: Formal quarantine with resolution plan - 3: Explicit tracking, status updates, and Occam's broom awareness Key insight: "Neither hidden nor allowed to destroy coherent framework" ## Anomaly → Hypothesis Flow Anomalies can SPAWN hypotheses (the "paradox grounding"): ``` Anomaly X1: "AB lineage shows position-dependent fates, but EMS doesn't" ↓ spawns → H3: Third Alternative (hybrid mechanism) ↓ X1.spawnedHypotheses = ["H-xxx-003"] H3.linkedAnomalies = ["X-xxx-001"] ``` ## CLI Commands - `brenner anomaly list [--status=active|resolved|deferred|paradigm_shifting]` - `brenner anomaly show ` - `brenner anomaly create --observation="..." --conflicts-with=H1,H2` - `brenner anomaly resolve --by=` - `brenner anomaly defer --reason="..."` - `brenner anomaly spawn-hypothesis ` → Creates linked hypothesis from anomaly ## Integration Points - **Hypothesis Registry**: Hypotheses gain `linkedAnomalies` (which anomalies they explain) - **Scorecard**: "Paradox Grounding" dimension now has data source - **Scorecard**: "Anomaly Quarantine Discipline" dimension evaluates this registry - **Dashboard**: Show active anomalies as "unresolved puzzles" ## Success Criteria - [ ] Anomaly schema defined with quarantine status - [ ] Conflict tracking (which hypotheses/assumptions challenged) - [ ] Resolution tracking (which hypothesis explains it) - [ ] Hypothesis spawning from anomalies - [ ] CLI commands for full CRUD + lifecycle - [ ] Integration with Hypothesis Registry ## Tasks (to be created) 1. Define Anomaly Zod Schema 2. Implement Anomaly Storage Layer 3. Implement Anomaly Lifecycle Transitions 4. Create Anomaly CLI Commands 5. Write Anomaly Module Unit Tests 6. Integrate with Hypothesis Registry (add linkedAnomalies, spawnedFrom)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:05:41.605610164-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T01:10:01.823360557-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T01:10:01.823360557-05:00","close_reason":"All 6 children tasks completed: schema, storage, lifecycle transitions, hypothesis spawning, CLI commands, and unit tests. +| `evidence add-excerpt --thread-id <id> ...` | Add an excerpt to an evidence record | ✅ |
+| `evidence list --thread-id <id>` | List evidence records in a pack | ✅ |
+| `evidence render --thread-id <id>` | Render evidence pack to markdown | ✅ |
+| `evidence post --thread-id <id> ...` | Post evidence summary to Agent Mail thread | ✅ |
+| `evidence verify --thread-id <id> ...` | Mark an evidence record as verified | ✅ |
 
 #### Config precedence (contract)
 
@@ -491,6 +498,110 @@ ntm broadcast $THREAD_ID "Please check your Agent Mail inbox"
 
 **Key insight**: Agents run in **your terminal** (via ntm), not in the cloud. You manage the sessions, review outputs, and decide when to compile. This is humans-in-the-loop orchestration.
 
+### Create and cite evidence packs
+
+Evidence packs let you import external sources (papers, datasets, prior session results) into a Brenner Loop session with stable IDs that can be cited in artifacts. This enables research on topics beyond just the Brenner transcripts.
+
+**Why evidence packs?**
+- Avoid model-memory hallucination by importing auditable evidence
+- Stable anchors (`EV-001`, `EV-001#E1`) for citation in artifacts
+- Excerpt-first: store only the snippets you actually use
+- Local-first: never ship copyrighted content to production
+
+**Evidence pack workflow:**
+
+```bash
+# 1. Pick a thread ID for your session
+export THREAD_ID="RS-20251231-cell-fate"
+
+# 2. Initialize an evidence pack
+./brenner.ts evidence init --thread-id $THREAD_ID
+
+# 3. Add a paper (auto-assigns EV-001)
+./brenner.ts evidence add \
+  --thread-id $THREAD_ID \
+  --type paper \
+  --title "Synaptic vesicle depletion dynamics" \
+  --source "doi:10.1234/neuro.2024.001" \
+  --relevance "Provides timescale data for H1" \
+  --supports H1
+
+# 4. Add a key excerpt from the paper (auto-assigns EV-001#E1)
+./brenner.ts evidence add-excerpt \
+  --thread-id $THREAD_ID \
+  --evidence-id EV-001 \
+  --text "Recovery time constant was 487 +/- 32 ms" \
+  --verbatim \
+  --location "p. 4, Results"
+
+# 5. Add a dataset
+./brenner.ts evidence add \
+  --thread-id $THREAD_ID \
+  --type dataset \
+  --title "Synthetic repetition benchmark v2" \
+  --source "file://benchmarks/synth_v2.json" \
+  --relevance "Test stimuli for T5 potency check" \
+  --informs T5
+
+# 6. Add prior session results
+./brenner.ts evidence add \
+  --thread-id $THREAD_ID \
+  --type prior_session \
+  --title "Initial hypothesis exploration" \
+  --source "session://RS-20251228-initial" \
+  --relevance "H2 was killed; avoid re-investigating" \
+  --refutes H2
+
+# 7. Mark evidence as verified
+./brenner.ts evidence verify \
+  --thread-id $THREAD_ID \
+  --evidence-id EV-001 \
+  --notes "Peer-reviewed in Nature Neuroscience"
+
+# 8. List and render the pack
+./brenner.ts evidence list --thread-id $THREAD_ID --json
+./brenner.ts evidence render --thread-id $THREAD_ID
+
+# 9. Post evidence summary to the Agent Mail thread
+./brenner.ts evidence post \
+  --thread-id $THREAD_ID \
+  --sender Operator \
+  --to BlueLake,PurpleMountain,GreenValley \
+  --subject "Evidence pack for $THREAD_ID"
+```
+
+**Evidence types:**
+| Type | Use case |
+|------|----------|
+| `paper` | Published research paper |
+| `preprint` | Unpublished manuscript |
+| `dataset` | Benchmark data, corpus, test stimuli |
+| `experiment` | Results from an experiment |
+| `observation` | Empirical observation |
+| `prior_session` | Results from another Brenner Loop session |
+| `expert_opinion` | Human expert statement |
+| `code_artifact` | Existing code as evidence |
+
+**Citing evidence in artifacts:**
+```markdown
+**Anchors**: §58, EV-001#E1 [inference]
+
+**Claim**: RRP depletion follows exponential decay (EV-001#E1, EV-002).
+
+| P1 | RRP decay rate | ~500ms (EV-001#E2) | ~200ms | indeterminate |
+```
+
+**File layout:**
+```
+artifacts/
+└── <thread_id>/
+    ├── artifact.md      # Compiled artifact
+    ├── evidence.json    # Evidence pack (structured)
+    └── evidence.md      # Evidence pack (human-readable)
+```
+
+See: `specs/evidence_pack_v0.1.md` for the full specification.
+
